quick_facts.label: Core qualification
quick_facts.value: Direct-entry state-service rules commonly require a BHMS (or equivalent) from a recognised homoeopathic medical institution; Tripura’s Grade-IV Medical Officer (Homoeo) rules are one current official example.

quick_facts.label: Registration
quick_facts.value: Registration/enrolment is a core eligibility condition. Tripura requires enrolment on its state register or the central register, while Delhi’s AYUSH Directorate states that registration with the appropriate registering body is mandatory for AYUSH practice.

quick_facts.label: BHMS preparation
quick_facts.value: The NCH BHMS programme is five years and six months, including a 12-month compulsory rotatory internship; it is designed to prepare graduates for health-care services in urban and rural areas.

quick_facts.label: Recruitment route
quick_facts.value: Appointments are employer- and state-specific. Delhi states that its AYUSH Medical Officers are recruited through UPSC using advertised vacancies and the Commission’s selection methods; other states may use their own public-service or health-department processes.

quick_facts.label: Service setting
quick_facts.value: The role is part of public AYUSH delivery. Odisha’s homoeopathic service-cadre restructuring specifically refers to health-care facilities in homoeopathic hospitals and dispensaries.

sections.heading: What a Homoeopathic Medical Officer does
paragraphs: A Homoeopathic Medical Officer is a government-employed registered homoeopathy practitioner whose work is centred on delivering homoeopathic care in the public system. The precise job description is set by the recruiting government or local body, but the professional foundation is the BHMS training standard: NCH describes the graduate as able to function in urban and rural health services and sets objectives spanning promotive, preventive, curative and rehabilitative aspects of common diseases.

In practice, this means outpatient clinical assessment and follow-up within the homoeopathic system, ethical professional conduct, and participation in the service functions assigned to the post. Government cadres can be based in homoeopathic dispensaries or hospitals; Odisha’s official restructuring order expressly frames its cadre around the functioning of both types of facilities.
bullets: Provide patient-facing homoeopathic services within the scope of the post and applicable registration and ethics rules.
Support preventive, promotive, curative and rehabilitative public-health objectives reflected in BHMS training.
Work in government homoeopathic dispensaries, hospitals, or other AYUSH service locations as assigned by the employer.
Follow the specific clinical, administrative, reporting and programme duties stated in the recruitment notice, service rules and departmental orders.

sections.heading: Entry route after BHMS
paragraphs: The usual route is: complete an NCH-regulated BHMS programme and its compulsory rotatory internship; obtain the required homoeopathy registration; then apply when a state/UT, local body or central-government-linked employer advertises Medical Officer vacancies. The eligibility details, including domicile, language, age, reservation, registration authority and selection method, differ by jurisdiction and advertisement.

For example, Tripura’s 2025 rules for Grade-IV Medical Officer (Homoeo) list a recognised BHMS or equivalent degree and registration on the Tripura state register or central register as essential qualifications; a postgraduate degree in homoeopathy is listed as desirable, not essential. Delhi says its AYUSH Medical Officers are recruited through UPSC after vacancies are advertised.
bullets: Complete BHMS: five years and six months, including the 12-month compulsory rotatory internship.
Ensure the degree is from a recognised institution and obtain the applicable state or national homoeopathy registration.
Monitor the relevant state public service commission, health department, AYUSH directorate, municipal/local body and UPSC recruitment channels.
Read each notification in full: local eligibility, age cut-off, category rules, examination/interview process and documentation can vary.

sections.heading: Knowledge and skills developed for the role
paragraphs: The role calls for the clinical and professional competencies embedded in BHMS education: homoeopathic case-taking, materia medica, organon/philosophy, practice of medicine, research methodology and biostatistics, together with awareness of professional ethics and the limits of homoeopathy in health care. NCH’s regulations also require graduates to recognise the applicability and limitations of homoeopathy and to learn integration of medical services for effective health-care delivery.

Because eligibility and practice depend on recognised qualification and registration, careful documentation, regulatory awareness and ethical conduct are as important as subject knowledge. The NCH Board of Ethics and Registration is responsible for the national register and for regulating professional conduct and medical ethics under the Act.
bullets: Homoeopathic case-taking and patient communication.
Clinical judgement within the recognised homoeopathic scope, including recognition of limitations and need for appropriate referral or integration.
Understanding of preventive and community-health objectives.
Record discipline, research literacy and basic biostatistics.
Professional ethics, registration compliance and familiarity with the employer’s service rules.

sections.heading: Where the role is performed
paragraphs: Workplace assignment depends on the sponsoring government. Official Odisha service documents identify homoeopathic hospitals and dispensaries as facilities served by the state homoeopathic medical cadre. This supports the node’s typical outpatient/public-service framing, but does not mean every appointment has the same site, hours, workload or contractual status.

Applicants should treat the individual recruitment notice and appointment order as controlling: they determine the employing authority, posting location, whether the post is regular or contractual, transfer liability, probation and duty schedule.
bullets: State AYUSH or health-department homoeopathic dispensaries.
Government homoeopathic hospitals.
District or local-government public-health facilities where the post is sanctioned.
Potential rural, urban or district postings, subject to the relevant service rules and vacancy notice.

sections.heading: Career progression
paragraphs: Promotion structures are state-cadre specific. Odisha provides a concrete official example: its December 2023 restructuring retained Homoeopathic Medical Officer as the base level and created a hierarchy through Senior HMO, District Homoeopathic Medical Officer/Deputy Superintendent/Chief Medical Officer roles, Deputy Director (Homoeopathy), and Joint Director (Homoeopathy). This is illustrative rather than a nationwide ladder.

A postgraduate degree may strengthen eligibility or competitiveness in some systems. In Tripura’s direct-recruitment rules, a postgraduate homoeopathy degree is desirable for Medical Officer (Homoeo), while BHMS and registration are the stated essentials.
bullets: Base role: Homoeopathic Medical Officer.
Possible state-cadre advancement: senior medical-officer and district supervisory/administrative posts.
Possible higher administration: deputy-director or joint-director level posts where such cadres exist.
Postgraduate homoeopathy study can be a desirable qualification, subject to the employer’s rules.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: Pay is not nationally uniform: it depends on the state/UT, cadre, whether the engagement is regular or contractual, pay-revision rules and allowances. As a dated example, Odisha’s 27 December 2023 cadre-restructuring resolution placed base-level Homoeopathic Medical Officer posts at ₹44,900–₹142,400 under Level 10 of the Odisha Revised Scales of Pay Rules, 2017; it placed Senior HMO and district-level promotional posts at ₹56,100–₹177,500 under Level 12. These figures should not be used as a current offer or as a salary benchmark for another state.
Candidates should rely on the pay level, consolidated remuneration (if contractual), allowances and terms printed in the specific vacancy advertisement and appointment order.
bullets: Compensation is jurisdiction- and appointment-type-specific.
Regular cadre posts may use the relevant state pay matrix and allowances.
Contractual/part-time engagements can have materially different remuneration and benefits.
Verify the dated advertisement rather than relying on historical pay figures.
